findings.title: Model credential guard accepts raw secrets under noncanonical env names
findings.description: rejectRawModelCredentials only checks rawApiKey and two exact model-key variable names. If createModelCredentialSource is handed an env object containing a provider-shaped secret under another variable name, construction succeeds and returns host-subscription provenance. The ticket requires any construction path handed a raw provider key to fail closed, so supplied env values need to be scanned and rejected, not just selected variable names.

findings.title: Unknown multi-segment provider tokens are not fail-closed
findings.description: The unknown-token fallback misses a provider-prefixed multi-segment shape with long numeric segments followed by a long alphabetic segment under a neutral metadata key. scanSecretLikeText reports no finding and redactSecretValues leaves the value unchanged, so the redactor still has a fail-open class for secret-shaped provider tokens.

findings.title: Trace redaction does not cover non-meta trace identifiers
findings.description: TraceProcessor redacts LogEvent.meta, but sessionId, correlationId, and upid are emitted unchanged. A generated provider-shaped value used as a correlation id is persisted raw in toJsonl. SEC-1 requires zero raw provider keys anywhere in JSONL trace artifacts, so trace identifiers must be validated or redacted before emission.
